pi_items.source: Web search and fetch; task_utils.sh comment confirms use of REAL websites (line 3-4); crawler-test.com is confirmed as a real, public test site used by SEO professionals for testing various website configurations including AMP implementations
Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh (line 28), and export_result.sh (line 13); directories are created by setup_task.sh (line 30)
Consistent across task.json, setup_task.sh (line 29, 36), and export_result.sh (line 14); directory created by setup_task.sh (line 31)

Web searches and Screaming Frog official documentation (screamingfrog.co.uk) do not document specific CSV column headers for AMP exports. The verifier script (line 47) only checks for presence of 'AMP' or 'Validation' in the header, not specific column names. No authoritative source confirms these exact column names exist in Screaming Frog's AMP export.

Verified through Screaming Frog official documentation (screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/issues/): 'Missing Canonical' (https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/issues/amp/amp-missing-canonical/), 'Missing Non-AMP Return Link' (https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/issues/amp/missing-non-amp-return-link/), and 'Validation Error' are all real AMP validation issues detected by Screaming Frog's AMP validator (which uses the official AMP validator)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real data from crawler-test.com, a legitimate, publicly-accessible SEO testing website that contains multiple AMP test case implementations. Screaming Frog SEO Spider will perform bulk AMP validation using the official AMP validator and can detect common AMP errors including Missing Canonical, Missing Non-AMP Return Link, and other Validation Errors. The task will create an AMP CSV export in ~/Documents/SEO/exports/ and an audit report in ~/Documents/SEO/reports/. The specific CSV column headers claimed in the metadata cannot be verified from official Screaming Frog documentation, but the export functionality and error detection capabilities are confirmed to exist.
